# RuleX

> RuleX turns Excel models into APIs. Upload a spreadsheet, map input and output cells, get a URL any application can call.

The business logic stays in Excel. If the spreadsheet gives the right answer, the API gives the right answer. RuleX handles authentication, validation, type conversion, and hosting.

## Getting Started

- [Overview](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/): What RuleX is and how it works — upload a Data Source, define a Schema, create an Endpoint.
- [Tutorial](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/tutorial/): Step-by-step walkthrough building a savings growth calculator: JSON API, data validations, fixed inputs, web form, tests, and array outputs.

## Core Concepts

- [Data Sources](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/concepts/data-sources/): An Excel file uploaded to RuleX. Immutable once created. Only static (non-formula) cells can be used as inputs. Supports `.xlsx` upload or Google Sheets import.
- [Endpoint Schemas](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/concepts/endpoint-schemas/): Maps named fields to cells in a Data Source. Defines the API contract — which inputs consumers send and which outputs they receive. Supports scalar, range, and spill range output types.
- [Endpoints](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/concepts/endpoints/): A URL path backed by a Schema. Operates in JSON API mode, Web Form mode, or both simultaneously. Returns HTTP 404 until the underlying Data Source finishes compiling.
- [Organisation](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/concepts/organisation/): All RuleX resources (Data Sources, Schemas, Endpoints, team members) belong to an Organisation and are isolated from others. Each Organisation gets a unique domain.

## Configuring Endpoints

- [JSON API Mode](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/configuring/json-api-mode/): Accept POST requests from any HTTP client. Requires an API Config for the API key. Request body is JSON; response is JSON. Auth header: `X-API-Key: <key>`.
- [API Config](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/configuring/api-config/): Holds the 64-character API key for JSON API Endpoints. One config can cover multiple Endpoints. Supports IP whitelisting.
- [Web Form Mode](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/configuring/web-form-mode/): Expose an Endpoint as an HTML form in the browser. Full access feature. Requires Web Form Credentials.
- [Web Form Credentials](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/configuring/web-form-credentials/): Username and password pairs that protect web form access. Full access feature. Separate from RuleX Admin accounts.
- [Web Form Themes](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/configuring/web-form-themes/): Customise web form appearance using daisyUI themes. Full access feature. Supports separate light and dark variants.
- [Fixed Inputs](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/fixed-inputs/): Values baked into an Endpoint at configuration time. Invisible to API consumers — they cannot see or override them. Useful for per-partner constants like commission rates or promotional rates.

## Reference

- [Data Validations](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/reference/data-validations/): RuleX enforces Excel Data Validation rules automatically. Invalid inputs are rejected before the model runs. JSON API returns HTTP 422 with field-level errors; web form shows inline errors. Supported types: whole number, decimal, list, date, text length, custom formula.
- [Type Casting](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/reference/type-casting/): How RuleX converts between JSON types and Excel types. Inputs: Text, Integer, Decimal, Boolean, Date (ISO 8601), Date & Time (ISO 8601). Outputs: numbers, booleans, strings, dates, formula errors, and 2D arrays for ranges.
- [Formula Errors](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/reference/formula-errors/): When an output cell contains a formula error (`#VALUE!`, `#DIV/0!`, `#REF!`, etc.), RuleX returns the error string in the response rather than failing the request.
- [Supported Formulas](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/reference/supported-formulas/): Full list of Excel formulas RuleX can evaluate. All behave identically to Excel 2021. Unsupported formulas cause the Data Source build to fail.
- [Excel Compatibility](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/reference/excel-compatibility/): RuleX follows Excel 2021 behaviour including dynamic arrays. Notes on differences when importing models from Google Sheets (ARRAYFORMULA not supported, FILTER syntax differs) or LibreOffice (CSE array formulas).

## Self-Hosting

- [Exporting Endpoints](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/self-hosting/exporting-endpoints/): Export JSON API Endpoints as a self-contained ZIP. Contains `server.py`, `rulex.zip` (Python package with WebAssembly modules), and a README. Self-contained — does not call back to RuleX servers. Suitable for air-gapped environments. Web Form Endpoints cannot be exported.
- [Running the Server](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/self-hosting/running-the-server/): Run `uv run server.py` to start the HTTP server on port 8000. Demo exports compile modules on first request (cold start). `rulex.zip` can also be used as a Python library. Supports `x86_64-linux` and `aarch64-linux`.

## Additional Features

- [Endpoint Tests](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/endpoint-tests/): Upload an Excel file where each row is a test case. RuleX runs every row through the model and returns a results file with outputs appended. Full access feature.
- [Team Management](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/team-management/): Invite members to your Organisation. Two access levels: Superuser (full access) and Non-superuser (granular permissions for Data Sources, Schemas, Endpoints, team, and organisation settings).
- [Full Access Features](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/full-access-features/): Web Form Mode, Web Form Themes, Web Form Credentials, and Endpoint Tests require full hosted access. Contact support@coverstack.in to upgrade.

## Architecture

- [Architecture](https://docs.rulex.coverstack.in/architecture/): RuleX transpiles Excel formulas to Rust, compiles to WebAssembly, then AOT-compiles to native executables. Each request is fully isolated. Runtime uses Wasmer with LLVM backend. Caching hints (Forever, Today, Never) are returned alongside each calculation result.
